source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative (SDR)
source_jobs.description:
American factories deserve a supply chain that moves as fast as they do. The next generation of American manufacturing is being built right now. Nox Metals is the supply chain behind it.

Nox Metals is a technology company in Detroit supplying aluminum plate, bar, and rounds to aerospace and defense manufacturers. We use software and automation to supply metal to American factories faster than the industry thought possible.

We need an Inside Sales hire to be the engine that turns RFQs into shipped orders. Quotes out fast. Specs locked in tight. Orders pushed cleanly into production. Customers in the loop every step of the way. The inbox is the front door of the company and you own it. Nox Metals is an employer with real opportunity for long term career growth, this is a place to build a career, not just hold a job.

You will:

 - Own inbound RFQs and quoting end to end, every quote out the door fast and accurate

 - Estimate jobs in WAYNE, run nests, validate scrap, price the work, and turn it into a quote the customer can act on

 - Translate customer drawings, specs, and POs into clean, complete jobs in NOX NEST

 - Push orders into production with every detail right, alloy, temper, thickness, tolerances, cert requirements, ship date, packaging

 - Work tightly with scheduling and the floor to commit dates we can hit and hit the dates we commit to

 - Own the customer relationship inside the four walls, every email, every quote follow-up, every order confirmation, every status update

 - Catch issues before they become problems, missing specs, mismatched POs, unrealistic dates, conflicting cert requirements

 - Coordinate with quality, supply chain, and shipping on every order from open to delivered

 - Work hand in hand with Outside Sales, you are the engine that lets them keep selling

 - Capture every quote, every win, every loss in our CRM and quoting system, the data is the moat

 - Always ask questions, never guess when something is unclear

 - Look at every quote, every order, every workflow and figure out how to make it better

 - Work safely every shift and hold your teammates to the same standard

You should be:

 - Experience in inside sales, quoting, estimating, or customer service in metals, machine shops, manufacturing, or comparable industrial environments

 - Strong technical fluency, you can read a drawing, talk alloys, tempers, tolerances, and lead times, and know what a part actually needs

 - Skilled at quoting and estimating, every line item priced right, every assumption documented

 - Cracked with AI tools, spreadsheets, modern software, and quoting tools, you live in our stack and you make it faster every week

 - Tightly partnered with software teams in past roles, comfortable writing specs, filing tickets, and shaping the tools you use

 - Comfortable on the phone, on email, and in customer portals all day, every day

 - Sharp with numbers and detail oriented, every quote, every PO, every line right the first time

 - High attention to quality, the quote, the order, the cert, the package

 - Always thinking about how to make processes better, you do not accept "this is how we have always done it"

 - Absolutely customer obsessed, you carry every customer's deadline like it is your own

 - A team player with a good attitude, you make the company better for everyone around you

 - Someone who takes ownership, if it is in your inbox or on your quote board, it is your responsibility

 - Precise under pressure and reliable, customers count on the dates and prices you commit to

 - Organized enough to juggle dozens of open quotes and orders without dropping one

 - Committed to safety, you follow every protocol, wear your PPE on the floor, and never cut corners that put people at risk

 - High agency, you handle big items alone and ask for help when needed

 - Low ego, you walk the floor, you talk to operators, you do the unglamorous work because it needs to get done

 - Not afraid to work outside normal

source_jobs.title: IT and SecOps
source_jobs.description:
IT and SecOps | Nox Metals | Detroit, MI

American factories deserve a supply chain that moves as fast as they do. The next generation of American manufacturing is being built right now. Nox Metals is the supply chain behind it.

Nox Metals is a technology company in Detroit supplying aluminum plate, bar, and rounds to aerospace and defense manufacturers. We use software and automation to supply metal to American factories faster than the industry thought possible.

We need an IT and SecOps lead to own every laptop, every account, every network, every endpoint, and every byte of customer data. Our customers are aerospace and defense manufacturers. Their security expectations are our floor, not our ceiling. Nox Metals is an employer with real opportunity for long term career growth, this is a place to build a career, not just hold a job.

You will:

 - Own IT and SecOps end to end as a solo IC across the entire company, office, factory, and field

 - Own CMMC compliance end to end, build it, run it, audit it, certify it, defend it

 - Own NIST 800-171 controls, DFARS 7012 cyber requirements, and all customer-driven security obligations

 - Build and maintain the System Security Plan (SSP), POA&M, and every artifact CMMC and customer audits demand

 - Own identity and access management, SSO, MFA, provisioning, deprovisioning, least privilege across every system

 - Own endpoint management, MDM, EDR, patching, encryption, and asset inventory across every laptop, desktop, and shop floor device

 - Own network infrastructure, firewalls, segmentation between IT and OT, VPN, Wi-Fi, and remote access

 - Own the OT side of the house, segment and harden CNC controllers, saw HMIs, and PLCs from the corporate network

 - Own cloud security across our SaaS stack, Supabase, GitHub, Microsoft 365, and every tool we run

 - Own backup, disaster recovery, and business continuity, test it, do not just document it

 - Run vulnerability management, log monitoring, and incident response, fix it once and fix it for good

 - Own employee onboarding and offboarding from an IT and security standpoint, day one access ready, day-of departure access cut

 - Run security training and phishing simulations across the company

 - Build dashboards and reports on patch compliance, endpoint health, identity hygiene, and incident metrics

 - Partner with the software team to embed security into NOX NEST, WAYNE, and our internal tools

 - Always ask questions, never guess when something is unclear

 - Look at every process and figure out how to make it better

 - Work safely every shift and hold your teammates to the same standard

You should be:

 - 3 to 4+ years owning IT and security in a manufacturing, defense, aerospace, or comparable regulated environment

 - Deep experience owning CMMC, NIST 800-171, and DFARS 7012 from the inside, you have built the SSP, run the controls, and passed assessments

 - Fluent in identity, endpoint, network, cloud, and OT security as a single integrated stack

 - Experienced segmenting IT and OT networks in a real factory, not just on paper

 - Comfortable as a solo IC, you own the function end to end and pick the tools we run on

 - Cracked with software, scripting, and automation, you do not click through 200 user accounts when a script will do it

 - Calm under pressure when an incident happens, methodical when running root cause

 - High attention to quality, every control, every log, every access review

 - Always thinking about how to make processes better, you do not accept "this is how we have always done it"

 - Absolutely customer obsessed, our security posture is how customers trust us with their data and their parts

 - A team player with a good attitude, you make the company better for everyone around you

 - Someone who takes ownership, if it has a chip in it, it is your responsibility

 - Precise under pressure and reliable, security does not bend for a deadline

 - Organized and detail oriented, audit-ready ev

source_jobs.title: Director of Software Engineering
source_jobs.description:
Director of Software Engineering | Nox Metals | Detroit, MI

American factories deserve a supply chain that moves as fast as they do. The next generation of American manufacturing is being built right now. Nox Metals is the supply chain behind it.

Nox Metals is a technology company in Detroit supplying aluminum plate, bar, and rounds to aerospace and defense manufacturers. We use software and automation to supply metal to American factories faster than the industry thought possible.

We need a Director of Software Engineering to lead the team building the systems that run the company. Our software is not a side project, it is the product. NOX NEST runs the factory floor, WAYNE runs nesting and quoting, Gondor runs the customer experience, MORIA runs inventory and demand intelligence. Every shipment we send goes out because of code we wrote. This role owns the engineering org, the roadmap, and the team that builds it. Nox Metals is an employer with real opportunity for long term career growth, this is a place to build a career, not just hold a job.

You will:

 - Lead, hire, and grow the engineering team across NOX NEST, WAYNE, Gondor, and MORIA

 - Own the engineering roadmap end to end, set priorities, ship on time

 - Stay hands on, write code, review PRs, pair with engineers on the hardest problems

 - Work directly with the founder to translate company strategy into engineering execution

 - Walk the floor every week and make sure the team is building what the business actually needs

 - Set the bar for code quality, system design, deploy hygiene, on call, and production reliability

 - Run the hiring loop, close candidates, and onboard new engineers fast

 - Drive real ML and AI features into production, cut time models, demand intelligence, AI parsing of sales orders, agentic workflows across the company

 - Own production systems, incident response, postmortems, and the culture of fix forward

 - Make the team faster every quarter, better tools, better workflows, better leverage on AI

 - Look at every system and figure out how to make it better

 - Work safely every shift and hold the team to the same standard

You should be:

 - 7+ years of engineering experience with 2+ years leading and managing a team

 - Track record of shipping production software at a startup, ideally scaled a team from a handful to 10+ engineers

 - Strong in TypeScript, Node, and modern web frameworks (NestJS, Next.js, React, or comparable)

 - Deep experience with Postgres, Supabase, or comparable database and backend platforms

 - Still writes production code and wants to

 - Cracked with AI tools, you use them every day and you have figured out how to make a whole team 10x more efficient with them

 - A builder at heart, you would rather ship a working v1 today than ship a perfect v3 next month, and you instill that in the team

 - Strong product instincts, you sit with a customer or an operator and turn what you see into a roadmap

 - A great manager of engineers, you grow people, you give hard feedback, you set a high bar, and people want to work for you

 - Comfortable working close to the metal, the floor, the customer, the operator, your team writes software that lives in the real world and you lead from the front

 - Relentless on quality, every PR, every deploy, every customer-facing experience

 - Always thinking about how to make systems better, you do not accept "this is how we have always done it"

 - Absolutely customer obsessed, every line of code your team ships eventually shows up as a part on a customer's dock

 - User obsessed, you sit with the operator, the scheduler, the buyer, you watch them work, and you build software they actually want to use

 - A team player with a good attitude, you make the team better for everyone around you

 - Someone who takes ownership of the whole engineering org, if it is in production, it is your responsibility

 - Precise under pressure and reliable

 - Organized and detail oriented

 -

source_jobs.title: Factory Shift Lead - Weekend
source_jobs.description:
Factory Shift Lead, Weekend | Nox Metals | Detroit, MI

American factories deserve a supply chain that moves as fast as they do. The next generation of American manufacturing is being built right now. Nox Metals is the supply chain behind it.

Nox Metals is a technology company in Detroit supplying aluminum plate, bar, and rounds to aerospace and defense manufacturers. We use software and automation to supply metal to American factories faster than the industry thought possible.

We need a Factory Shift Lead to own the weekend shift and keep the floor running while the rest of the country is off. American factories do not stop, and neither do we. Nox Metals is an employer with real opportunity for long term career growth, this is a place to build a career, not just hold a job.

Shift hours: Saturday and Sunday, 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM.

You will:

 - Lead the weekend shift team, set the pace, own the outcome, execute a clean handoff

 - Take a clean handoff from the weekday team and leave the floor ready for Monday

 - Operate industrial saws, bandsaws, CNC equipment, and material handling systems

 - Load, measure, cut, and inspect aluminum plate, bar, and rounds to spec

 - Run forklifts and overhead cranes

 - Follow cut lists and production schedules with precision

 - Receive inbound material and verify against purchase orders

 - Package, label, and stage outbound shipments

 - Record cut data and job completions in our software systems, accurately, every time

 - Always ask questions, never guess when something is unclear

 - Look at every process and figure out how to make it better

 - Keep the floor clean, organized, and ready for the next job

 - Work safely every shift and hold your teammates to the same standard

You are:

 - Knowledgeable or experienced in a factory, metals service center, bandsaws, industrial saws, CNC equipment, or skilled trades

 - A natural leader, you set the standard for the shift and people follow because of how you work, not because of a title

 - Committed to safety, you follow every protocol, wear your PPE, and never cut corners that put people at risk

 - High attention to quality, every cut, every cert, every shipment

 - Always thinking about how to make processes better, you do not accept "this is how we have always done it"

 - Absolutely customer obsessed, delivery timelines matter because customers are counting on them

 - A team player with a good attitude, you make the shift better for everyone around you

 - Someone who takes ownership, if it is your shift, it is your responsibility

 - Precise under pressure and reliable, the weekend runs because you show up ready

 - Self-directed, you can run the floor with a smaller team and keep production moving

 - Comfortable with software, we track everything digitally and you will too

 - Organized and detail oriented

 - High agency, you handle big items alone and ask for help when needed

 - Low ego, you do the unglamorous work because it needs to get done

 - Able to lift up to 50 lbs and operate heavy equipment on your feet for a full shift

 - Happy to work weekends as your primary schedule

 - Not afraid to work outside normal hours when America demands it

 - Never says "that's not my job"

Nice to have:

 - Experience in metals, aluminum, or aerospace/defense supply chains

 - Forklift and/or overhead crane certification

 - Prior shift lead or supervisory experience in manufacturing

 - Comfortable using AI tools to work faster and smarter

This role is full time, in person in Detroit.

source_jobs.title: Director of Manufacturing Operations
source_jobs.description:
Director of Manufacturing Operations | Nox Metals | Detroit, MI

American factories deserve a supply chain that moves as fast as they do. The next generation of American manufacturing is being built right now. Nox Metals is the supply chain behind it.

Nox Metals is a technology company in Detroit supplying aluminum plate, bar, and rounds to aerospace and defense manufacturers. We use software and automation to supply metal to American factories faster than the industry thought possible.

This is one of the most important hires we will ever make. We need a Director of Manufacturing Operations to run the entire factory. Every shift, every machine, every operator, every cut, every shipment. The factory is the company. If it runs, we win. If it does not, nothing else matters.

We are looking for someone with the discipline, presence, and bias for action of a military operator. Someone who walks the floor like they own it, runs a tight ship across all four shifts, and makes decisions in the moment without flinching. Nox Metals is an employer with real opportunity for long term career growth, this is a place to build a career, not just hold a job.

You will:

 - Own end-to-end manufacturing operations across day, evening, night, and weekend shifts

 - Run a 24/7 factory with clear standards, clear accountability

 - Manage all shift leads and operators, set the standard, hold the line

 - Own production throughput, on-time delivery, quality, scrap rate, and safety performance

 - Drive every operational metric that matters and make them visible across the company every day

 - Walk the floor every single day, you are not a director who lives in an office

 - Run daily production meetings, shift handoffs, and weekly operational reviews

 - Build and enforce SOPs, work instructions, safety protocols, and quality procedures

 - Own factory layout, capacity planning, and equipment utilization across every machine and cell

 - Partner with engineering and software to deploy automation, AI tools, and process improvements on the floor

 - Own DFARS, AMS, ASTM, and customer-specific quality compliance, every cert, every shipment

 - Lead incident response and root cause analysis when something breaks, fix it once and fix it for good

 - Build the team, hire shift leads and operators, develop them, hold them accountable, move them up

 - Look at every process, every machine, every minute and figure out how to make it better

 - Work safely every shift and hold the entire factory to the same standard

You should be:

 - 4 to 5+ years running an entire factory or major production operation, this is non-negotiable

 - Military, defense manufacturing, aerospace, or high-output industrial leadership background strongly preferred

 - A natural leader with command presence, people follow you because of how you operate, not because of a title

 - Fluent in metals, machining, CNC, sawing, or comparable precision manufacturing environments

 - Disciplined, organized, and process-driven, you build systems that run without you

 - Bias for action, you make the call in the moment with the information you have

 - High attention to quality, every cut, every cert, every shipment, every safety standard

 - Always thinking about how to make processes better, you do not accept "this is how we have always done it"

 - Absolutely customer obsessed, every customer commitment runs through your factory

 - Cracked with software and data, you run the factory by the numbers, not by feel

 - Committed to safety, you follow every protocol, wear your PPE, and never cut corners that put people at risk

 - A team player with a good attitude, you make the company better for everyone around you

 - Someone who takes ownership, the factory is yours, end to end

 - Precise under pressure and reliable, the company runs because you show up ready

 - High agency, you handle big items alone and ask for help when needed

 - Low ego, you walk the floor, you wear the boots, you do

source_jobs.title: Perception Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Perception Engineer | Nox Metals | Detroit, MI

American factories deserve a supply chain that moves as fast as they do. The next generation of American manufacturing is being built right now. Nox Metals is the supply chain behind it.

Nox Metals is a technology company in Detroit supplying aluminum plate, bar, and rounds to aerospace and defense manufacturers. We use software and automation to supply metal to American factories faster than the industry thought possible.

We need a Perception Engineer to give the factory eyes, ears, and a sense of touch. Vision systems on every machine. Vibration sensors on every saw. Thermal sensors on every cut. Every signal turned into data, every dataset turned into a better cost model, a smarter nest, a tighter cycle, and a more reliable cell. Nox Metals is an employer with real opportunity for long term career growth, this is a place to build a career, not just hold a job.

You will:

 - Own the perception stack across the entire factory, vision, vibration, thermal, force, and any other sensor that gives us a signal worth capturing

 - Build vision systems for robotics, inspection, dimensional verification, defect detection, and material identification

 - Build sensor systems on saws, CNC equipment, and automated cells, vibration for blade wear, thermal for cut conditions, current draw for load, and whatever else makes the cell smarter

 - Feed perception data into job costing, cycle time prediction, and nesting optimization, the perception layer is what makes WAYNE and our cost models actually accurate

 - Own the full pipeline end to end, sensor selection, mounting, calibration, data collection, labeling, training, deployment, edge inference, monitoring, retraining

 - Build edge inference where it makes sense and cloud inference where it makes sense, you make the call

 - Build the data pipelines that capture every frame, every waveform, every reading, the data that powers our ML and operational intelligence

 - Partner tightly with the ML, automation, controls, and software teams, you sit at the seam where hardware, signals, models, and software all meet

 - Run experiments, ablations, and real-world tests to prove what works on the floor, not just on a benchmark

 - Always ask questions, never guess when something is unclear

 - Look at every sensor, every model, every signal, and figure out how to make it better

 - Work safely every shift and hold your teammates to the same standard

You should be:

 - Strong technical foundation in computer vision, signal processing, and sensor systems, judged on capability not years

 - Hands-on with industrial cameras, machine vision platforms, and 2D and 3D imaging

 - Experienced with non-vision sensors, accelerometers, vibration, thermal, current, force, and the analog and digital interfaces that connect them

 - Fluent in modern ML and DL frameworks (PyTorch, JAX, OpenCV, or comparable) and the surrounding tooling

 - Comfortable shipping the full pipeline, you collect the data, you train the model, you deploy it on edge or cloud, you monitor it, and you retrain it

 - Experienced integrating perception into PLCs, robotics, and production software systems

 - Comfortable on the software side, you write the integration, hit the API, structure the payload, reason about the model in production

 - Tightly partnered with ML, automation, and controls teams in past roles, comfortable writing specs, filing tickets, and shaping the tools you use

 - Cracked with AI tools, modern software, and data, you build perception systems that talk to the rest of the company

 - A builder at heart, you would rather ship a working v1 today than ship a perfect v3 next month

 - High attention to quality, every model, every dataset, every sensor calibration

 - Always thinking about how to make systems better, you do not accept "this is how we have always done it"

 - Absolutely customer obsessed, every signal you capture eventually shows up as a part on a custo

source_jobs.title: Local Delivery Driver (CDL A Preferred)
source_jobs.description:
American factories deserve a supply chain that moves as fast as they do. The next generation of American manufacturing is being built right now. Nox Metals is the supply chain behind it.

We use software and automation to supply metal to American factories faster than the industry thought possible.

We are looking for a Delivery Driver who treats every load and every customer like the company's reputation rides with them, because it does.

HOURS: 6AM - 3PM (flexible)

You will:

 - Deliver aluminum plate, bar, and rounds to customers safely and on time, every time

 - Strap and secure every load correctly so nothing shifts, moves, or gets damaged in transit

 - Operate the truck with a clean driving record and a safety first mindset on every route

 - Greet every customer with a smile and represent Nox like an owner at every dock

 - Keep your truck clean, fueled, organized, and ready to run

 - Jump in on the floor between routes to load, move, and prep material whenever the work needs it

You should be:

 - A safe, reliable driver with a clean record. A CDL A is preferred, or willingness to obtain one.

 - Happy to grab a strap, a broom, or a heavy load whenever it needs doing, and someone who never says "that's not my job"

 - The kind of person every customer is glad to see, professional and friendly at every stop

 - High agency and organized, able to plan and run your routes without being managed

 - Comfortable jumping into physical labor on the floor when you are not behind the wheel, and glad to do it

 - Low ego, hard working, and ready to go the extra distance to get material to a factory on time

 - Not afraid to work outside normal hours when a customer needs material to keep their line running

 - Mission driven and proud to be part of rebuilding American manufacturing

This role is full time, in person in Detroit.

source_jobs.title: Outside Sales
source_jobs.description:
Outside Sales | Nox Metals | Detroit, MI

American factories deserve a supply chain that moves as fast as they do. The next generation of American manufacturing is being built right now. Nox Metals is the supply chain behind it.

Nox Metals is a technology company in Detroit supplying aluminum plate, bar, and rounds to aerospace and defense manufacturers. We use software and automation to supply metal to American factories faster than the industry thought possible.

We need an Outside Sales hire to win new business and own accounts the rest of the industry will not touch. The job is machine shops first, the precision shops, the job shops, the contract manufacturers cutting parts for aerospace, defense, and industrial customers. You will walk their floors, prove Nox is faster and more reliable than anything they have used before, and win their material spend. Nox Metals is an employer with real opportunity for long term career growth, this is a place to build a career, not just hold a job.

You will:

 - Win new business across machine shops, precision job shops, and contract manufacturers, this is the core of the role

 - Pick up OEM, prime, and tier 1 accounts opportunistically when relationships open the door, but the day-to-day is shop floors

 - Own the full sales cycle, prospecting, first call, plant visit, quote, close, and ongoing account management

 - Walk machine shop floors, sit with owners, buyers, programmers, and engineers, understand the parts they make and the metal they need

 - Manage every account you close, you do not throw a customer over the wall after the first PO

 - Build deep relationships with shop owners, buyers, programmers, and ops leaders at every account

 - Bring the customer's voice back to operations, supply chain, software, and quality every single day

 - Quote fast, follow up faster, and close on the dates you commit to

 - Own a pipeline in our CRM, every conversation logged, every commitment tracked, every forecast honest

 - Travel as needed, in a region or nationally, judged on the relationships you build and the deals you close

 - Always ask questions, never guess when something is unclear

 - Look at every account and figure out how to win more of their wallet

 - Work safely on every customer floor and hold yourself to the same standard we hold our team to

You should be:

 - 3 to 5+ years of outside sales experience, ideally selling metals, industrial products, tooling, or technical components into machine shops

 - A new business hunter who can also run the account, you do not need a separate role to keep the customer happy

 - Comfortable on a shop floor, you can stand at a CNC, talk to the operator, and earn the buyer's trust before you ever quote a part

 - Skilled at building real relationships, customers should know your name, take your call, and want to give you more business

 - Strong technical fluency, you can talk alloys, tempers, tolerances, and lead times like a programmer

 - Cracked with AI tools, modern software, and CRM data, you run your pipeline like an operator, not like a guesser

 - A team player with a good attitude, you bring deals back to the company and the company makes them happen

 - High attention to quality, every quote, every commitment, every customer interaction

 - Always thinking about how to make processes better, you do not accept "this is how we have always done it"

 - Absolutely customer obsessed, you carry every customer's deadline like it is your own

 - Someone who takes ownership, if it is your account, it is your responsibility

 - Precise under pressure and reliable, customers count on the dates you commit to

 - Organized enough to run dozens of active opportunities and accounts without dropping one

 - Committed to safety, you follow every protocol on every customer floor and hold our team to the same standard

 - High agency, you handle big items alone and ask for help when needed

 - Low ego, you do the unglamorous work because it

source_jobs.title: ML Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ML Engineer | Nox Metals | Detroit, MI

American factories deserve a supply chain that moves as fast as they do. The next generation of American manufacturing is being built right now. Nox Metals is the supply chain behind it.

Nox Metals is a technology company in Detroit supplying aluminum plate, bar, and rounds to aerospace and defense manufacturers. We use software and automation to supply metal to American factories faster than the industry thought possible.

We need an ML Engineer to build the intelligence layer of the company. Cut-time models, nesting scoring, demand intelligence, sales order parsing, dimensional vision, cert OCR, and the next ten things we have not built yet. Every machine, every order, every shipment generates data, and you turn it into a moat. Nox Metals is an employer with real opportunity for long term career growth, this is a place to build a career, not just hold a job.

You will:

 - Own ML end to end across applied production work, novel research, and production deployment

 - Build cut-time models that predict how long every cut takes by alloy, thickness, geometry, and machine

 - Build nesting scoring and optimization models that make WAYNE smarter every day

 - Build demand intelligence and procurement models that tell us what to buy, when, and at what price

 - Build NLP and LLM features for sales order parsing, RFQ ingestion, and customer communication

 - Build vision and OCR systems for cert package automation and dimensional inspection

 - Ship features end to end, model, API, UI, deploy, monitor, no handoffs, no waiting

 - Own the full ML lifecycle, data collection, labeling, training, evaluation, deployment, monitoring, retraining

 - Build the data infrastructure and feature pipelines that future ML work depends on

 - Partner with the software team to embed ML into NOX NEST, WAYNE, and Gondor

 - Walk the floor, talk to operators, schedulers, and buyers, your training data starts where the work happens

 - Always ask questions, never guess when something is unclear

 - Look at every system and figure out how to make it better

 - Work safely every shift and hold your teammates to the same standard

You should be:

 - 5+ years of ML engineering experience shipping production models to real users

 - Deep technical foundation across applied ML, foundation models or novel architectures, and production deployment

 - Strong in Python, modern ML frameworks (PyTorch, JAX, or comparable), and the surrounding tooling

 - Comfortable shipping production code, you build the API, the integration, and the UI when that is what the job needs

 - Fluent in MLOps, model monitoring, retraining, and the unglamorous work of keeping models alive in production

 - Experienced collecting, labeling, and structuring training data from messy real-world systems

 - Cracked with AI tools, modern frameworks, and data, you move 10x faster than engineers who do not

 - A builder at heart, you would rather ship a working v1 today than ship a perfect v3 next month

 - High attention to quality, every model, every dataset, every deployment

 - Always thinking about how to make systems better, you do not accept "this is how we have always done it"

 - Absolutely customer obsessed, every model eventually shows up as a part on a customer's dock

 - User obsessed, you sit with the operator, the scheduler, the buyer, you watch them work, and you build models they actually trust

 - A team player with a good attitude, you make the team better for everyone around you

 - Someone who takes ownership, if it is in production, it is your responsibility

 - Precise under pressure and reliable

 - Organized and detail oriented

 - Committed to safety, you follow every protocol, wear your PPE, and never cut corners that put people at risk

 - High agency, you handle big items alone and ask for help when needed

 - Low ego, you walk the floor, you talk to operators, you do the unglamorous work because it needs to get done

 - No

source_jobs.title: Factory Shift Lead - Evening
source_jobs.description:
Factory Shift Lead, Evening | Nox Metals | Detroit, MI

American factories deserve a supply chain that moves as fast as they do. The next generation of American manufacturing is being built right now. Nox Metals is the supply chain behind it.

Nox Metals is a technology company in Detroit supplying aluminum plate, bar, and rounds to aerospace and defense manufacturers. We use software and automation to supply metal to American factories faster than the industry thought possible.

We need a Factory Shift Lead to own the evening shift, set the pace, and execute clean handoffs both directions. Nox Metals is an employer with real opportunity for long term career growth, this is a place to build a career, not just hold a job.

Shift hours: Monday through Friday, 3:30 PM to 12:00 AM.

You will:

 - Lead the evening shift team, set the pace, own the outcome, execute a clean handoff

 - Take a clean handoff from day shift and leave a clean handoff for the next morning

 - Operate industrial saws, bandsaws, CNC equipment, and material handling systems

 - Load, measure, cut, and inspect aluminum plate, bar, and rounds to spec

 - Run forklifts and overhead cranes

 - Follow cut lists and production schedules with precision

 - Receive inbound material and verify against purchase orders

 - Package, label, and stage outbound shipments

 - Record cut data and job completions in our software systems, accurately, every time

 - Always ask questions, never guess when something is unclear

 - Look at every process and figure out how to make it better

 - Keep the floor clean, organized, and ready for the next job

 - Work safely every shift and hold your teammates to the same standard

You are:

 - Knowledgeable or experienced in a factory, metals service center, bandsaws, industrial saws, CNC equipment, or skilled trades

 - A natural leader, you set the standard for the shift and people follow because of how you work, not because of a title

 - Committed to safety, you follow every protocol, wear your PPE, and never cut corners that put people at risk

 - High attention to quality, every cut, every cert, every shipment

 - Always thinking about how to make processes better, you do not accept "this is how we have always done it"

 - Absolutely customer obsessed, delivery timelines matter because customers are counting on them

 - A team player with a good attitude, you make the shift better for everyone around you

 - Someone who takes ownership, if it is your shift, it is your responsibility

 - Precise under pressure and reliable, evening shift runs because you show up ready

 - Comfortable with software, we track everything digitally and you will too

 - Organized and detail oriented

 - High agency, you handle big items alone and ask for help when needed

 - Low ego, you do the unglamorous work because it needs to get done

 - Able to lift up to 50 lbs and operate heavy equipment on your feet for a full shift

 - Not afraid to work outside normal hours when America demands it

 - Never says "that's not my job"

Nice to have:

 - Experience in metals, aluminum, or aerospace/defense supply chains

 - Forklift and/or overhead crane certification

 - Prior shift lead or supervisory experience in manufacturing

 - Comfortable using AI tools to work faster and smarter

This role is full time, in person in Detroit.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineering Intern
source_jobs.description:
American factories deserve a supply chain that moves as fast as they do. The next generation of American manufacturing is being built right now. Nox Metals is the supply chain behind it.

We use software and automation to supply metal to American factories faster than the industry thought possible.

Nox Metals went thru YC Summer 2025.

Software Engineer Intern

This is a real software engineering internship. You will own meaningful projects from design to deployment, and the code you write will run on the factory floor. No coffee runs, no shadowing. Your work ships to production and controls how real metal gets cut on real machines.

You will:

 - Design, build, test, and ship features across the systems that run our factory, from the floor command center to the nesting engine to the customer portal

 - Write code that deploys to the floor and controls how metal gets cut, tracked, and delivered

 - Debug, profile, and harden production systems that cannot go down in the middle of a shift

 - Sit next to the engineers and operators running the machines and watch your software hit the real world the same day you ship it

 - Take big problems off senior engineers' plates and own them end to end

You should be:

 - Pursuing a degree in computer science, software engineering, or a related field, or able to prove you can build without one

 - Strong in data structures, algorithms, and at least one language (Python, TypeScript, Go, C++, whatever you reach for)

 - Comfortable with Git and the basics of how web and distributed systems work

 - Fast, organized, and high agency, able to take a big item and run it down alone while asking for help when you need it

 - Cracked with AI tools and always hunting for a faster way to do the work

 - Low ego, customer obsessed, and never the person who says "that's not my job"

 - Willing to work outside normal hours when the floor needs it

Our stack: Python, TypeScript, React, Next.js, Node.js, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Docker, and AWS. You will not know all of it. We will teach you what you do not.

This role is full time, in person in Detroit. We are open to in person Windsor or Remote.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
American factories deserve a supply chain that moves as fast as they do. The next generation of American manufacturing is being built right now. Nox Metals is the supply chain behind it.

We use software and automation to supply metal to American factories faster than the industry thought possible.

We are hiring a Software Engineer in Detroit to build the platform that runs our factories. Our stack is TypeScript end to end: NestJS, Next.js, React, and Supabase. You will write the software that quotes orders, nests parts, drives machines, manages inventory, and gives customers a live window into their work. You will ship to production constantly and watch your code move metal on the floor the same day.

You will:

 - Own features end to end across the stack, from database schema to the screen

 - Build and extend our core systems: the factory command platform, the nesting and quoting engine, the customer portal, and inventory and demand intelligence

 - Wire software directly into saws, automation, and shop floor hardware

 - Turn messy real world operations into clean systems that run themselves

 - Use AI tooling aggressively to ship more in a day than most engineers ship in a week

 - Spend real time on the factory floor so you understand the problems you are solving

 - Take big, ambiguous problems and bring them all the way to done without being told how

You should be:

 - High agency and able to own large items alone, while asking for help when you need it

 - Fast. You feel urgency and you move with it

 - Organized enough to keep a lot of moving pieces straight at once

 - Raw smart, with the horsepower to pick up new domains quickly

 - Cracked with AI tools and always finding new ways to push them further

 - Low ego and obsessed with the customer

 - The kind of person who takes work off other people's plates and never says "that's not my job"

 - Always bringing new ideas, often from outside the world of metals and manufacturing

 - Mission driven, the kind of person who could see themselves building this for the next 30 years

 - Wanting to be working outside normal hours to pursue our mission

This role is full time, in person in Detroit.

Nox Metals is a technology company supplying aluminum plate, bar, and rounds to aerospace and defense manufacturers. We use software and automation to supply metal to American factories faster than the industry thought possible.

We need a software engineer on the ground in LA to build the systems that run our second factory.

You will:

 - Build production systems for quoting, scheduling, procurement, machine operations, and material tracking

 - Ship code that runs live on the factory floor

 - Build internal tools for automation, planning, and inventory across multiple alloy types

 - Build customer-facing tools for quoting, ordering, and order visibility

 - Work side by side with operators and machinists to find bottlenecks and ship fixes the same day

 - Own the technical foundation of a brand new facility from day one

 - Coordinate with the Detroit software team to keep platforms aligned across factories

You should be:

 - A strong full-stack engineer who ships fast and ships often

 - Comfortable building systems from zero

 - Cracked with AI tools. You move 10x faster because of them

 - Energized by ambiguity. You figure it out

 - Interested in software that moves real material, not pixels

 - High agency. You handle big items alone and ask for help when you need it

 - Low ego. You will spend time on the floor with operators and customers

 - Customer obsessed. Quality matters because real parts go to real factories

 - Not afraid to work outside normal hours when something needs to ship

source_jobs.title: Operations Lead
source_jobs.description:
Operations Lead | Nox Metals | Los Angeles, CA

American factories deserve a supply chain that moves as fast as they do. The next generation of American manufacturing is being built right now. Nox Metals is the supply chain behind it.

Nox Metals is a technology company supplying aluminum plate, bar, and rounds to aerospace and defense manufacturers. We use software and automation to supply metal to American factories faster than the industry thought possible.

We need an Operations Lead to stand up our LA factory from an empty building to a running production floor.

You will:

 - Own the build out of the LA facility from day one

 - Source and negotiate equipment, tooling, racking, material handling, and everything else the floor needs

 - Hire the LA team. Operators, technicians, drivers, and support roles

 - Build the systems and processes that keep the floor moving. Shift schedules, safety, quality, inventory, shipping

 - Coordinate with the Detroit team to bring proven processes to LA and improve them

 - Work directly with the software team to deploy NOX NEST on the floor and make sure it actually works for operators

 - Own throughput, on time delivery, and customer quality from the LA site

 - Lead the team day to day once the facility is running

You should be:

 - High agency. You see what needs to happen and you make it happen

 - A builder. You have stood things up from nothing before

 - A leader people want to work for. You set the pace and the standard

 - Organized. You can hold a build out, a hiring pipeline, and a production schedule in your head at once

 - Cracked with AI tools. You use them to move faster than people expect

 - Comfortable on the floor and comfortable with software

 - Low ego. You will run a forklift, mop a floor, and interview a candidate in the same day

 - Customer obsessed. Quality matters because real parts go to real factories

 - Not afraid to work outside normal hours when something needs to ship

 - The kind of person who never says "that's not my job"

This role is full time, in person in LA.

source_jobs.title: Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
Product Manager | Nox Metals | Detroit, MI

American factories deserve a supply chain that moves as fast as they do. The next generation of American manufacturing is being built right now. Nox Metals is the supply chain behind it.

Nox Metals is a technology company in Detroit supplying aluminum plate, bar, and rounds to aerospace and defense manufacturers. We use software and automation to supply metal to American factories faster than the industry thought possible.

We need a Product Manager who can take an idea and turn it into something running on the floor by the end of the week. NOX NEST, WAYNE, and Gondor are the products that run the company. Your job is to make the path from idea to production as short as possible. Nox Metals is an employer with real opportunity for long term career growth, this is a place to build a career, not just hold a job.

You will:

 - Own product end to end across NOX NEST, WAYNE, and Gondor, idea, spec, build, deploy, rollout, iterate

 - Walk every new feature through the factory yourself, sit with operators, schedulers, and buyers, and watch them use what we shipped

 - Write tight specs that engineers can build from without ten clarification meetings

 - Prioritize ruthlessly, say no to good ideas to ship great ones faster

 - Code lightly when it helps, write a script, build a quick prototype, ship a small change yourself

 - Run weekly product reviews and own the operating cadence end to end

You should be:

 - 3+ years of product management experience shipping real software to real users

 - Strong systems thinker who can decompose a fuzzy problem and write a spec an engineer can build from immediately

 - Comfortable in code, you do not need to be a senior engineer, but you can read a PR and ship a small change

 - Bias for shipping, you would rather get a v1 live today than design a perfect v3 next month

 - Comfortable on the floor, you sit at a saw, talk to a scheduler, walk a customer through the portal

 - Cracked with AI tools, modern software, and data, you live in the stack and you make it faster every week

 - Absolutely customer obsessed, every feature ends up on a customer's dock

 - User obsessed, you build features operators, schedulers, buyers, and customers actually want to use

 - High agency, low ego, fast, organized, and relentless under pressure

 - Committed to safety, you follow every protocol on the floor and never cut corners that put people at risk

 - Not afraid to work outside normal hours when America demands it

 - Never says "that's not my job"

Nice to have:

 - Background managing product in manufacturing, logistics, supply chain, or industrial environments

 - Experience shipping software for shop floors or other physical operations

 - Light coding background, scripting, SQL, or shipping prototypes yourself

 - Comfortable using AI tools to work faster and smarter

This role is full time, in person in Detroit.

Nice to have:

 - Forklift and/or overhead crane certification

 - Background in a fast-paced manufacturing environment

 - Comfortable using AI tools to work faster and smarter

This role is full time, in person in Detroit.

source_jobs.title: Material Handler
source_jobs.description:
Material Handler | Nox Metals | Detroit, MI

American factories deserve a supply chain that moves as fast as they do. The next generation of American manufacturing is being built right now. Nox Metals is the supply chain behind it.

Nox Metals is a technology company in Detroit supplying aluminum plate, bar, and rounds to aerospace and defense manufacturers. We use software and automation to supply metal to American factories faster than the industry thought possible.

We need a Material Handler on day shift who puts safety first, every shift, no exceptions. This is a role for someone who takes OSHA standards seriously, runs a forklift with precision, and wants to grow into bandsaw and metals operations over time.

Shift hours: Monday through Friday, 7:00 AM to 3:30 PM.

You will:

 - Follow all OSHA and site safety protocols without exception, and hold your teammates to the same standard

 - Operate a forklift to receive, stage, and move aluminum plate, bar, and rounds throughout the facility

 - Load and unload trucks safely and efficiently

 - Receive inbound material and verify against purchase orders

 - Package, label, and stage outbound shipments

 - Keep the floor clean, organized, and free of hazards

 - Record material movements accurately in our software systems

 - Always ask questions, never guess when something is unclear

 - Complete required safety training and certifications as assigned

 - Learn baseline operations of the bandsaw and other metals equipment as you grow in the role

You are:

 - Safety-obsessed. You wear your PPE, follow every protocol, and never cut corners that put people or product at risk

 - Trained in OSHA standards, or ready and eager to complete training

 - Experienced operating a forklift, or a fast learner ready to get certified

 - High attention to detail, every load, every label, every shipment

 - Coachable and curious, you want to learn the bandsaw and broader floor operations

 - A team player with a good attitude, you make the shift better for everyone around you

 - Someone who takes ownership, if it is your shift, it is your responsibility

 - Reliable and steady under pressure, day shift sets the pace for the whole operation

 - Comfortable with software, or willing to learn, we track everything digitally

 - Organized and dependable

 - Low ego, you do the unglamorous work because it needs to get done

 - Able to lift up to 50 lbs and operate heavy equipment on your feet for a full shift

 - Never says "that's not my job"

Nice to have:

 - Prior experience in a manufacturing, warehouse, or industrial environment

 - Forklift and/or overhead crane certification already in hand

 - Experience with bandsaws, industrial saws, or metals handling

 - Comfortable using AI tools to work faster and smarter

This role is full time, in person in Detroit.

source_jobs.title: Factory Technician - (BANDSAW Experience WANTED!)
source_jobs.description:
Bandsaw Operator

Detroit, MI · Full-Time · Competitive Pay + Equity

NOX Metals is a technology company in Detroit supplying aluminum plate, bar, and rounds to aerospace and defense manufacturers. We use software and automation to move metal faster than the industry thought possible. We run 24/7 and we are growing. If you can operate a bandsaw and take pride in cutting it right the first time, we want to talk.

You will:

- Operate industrial bandsaws to cut aluminum plate, bar, and rounds to customer spec

- Read and execute cut lists accurately — every cut, every order

- Measure and verify finished pieces against job requirements

- Load and unload material using forklifts and overhead cranes

- Inspect cuts for quality and flag any non-conforming material

- Package, label, and stage finished orders for shipment

- Perform basic machine maintenance and keep your work area clean

- Log job completions in our software system



You are:

- Experienced on industrial bandsaws or similar cutting equipment

- Able to read a tape measure and work to tight tolerances

- Detail-oriented — you do not ship a bad cut

- Reliable and physically capable of working on your feet for a full shift

- Someone who takes ownership of their machine and their work



Nice to have:

- Experience cutting aluminum or other non-ferrous metals

- Forklift and/or overhead crane certification

- Familiarity with aerospace or defense supply chain quality standards



Competitive pay + company equity. Apply at jobs.ashbyhq.com/nox-metals http://jobs.ashbyhq.com/nox-metals

notes: Nox Metals is a technology company in Detroit supplying aluminum plate, bar, and rounds to aerospace and defense manufacturers. They use software and automation to supply metal to American factories faster than the industry thought possible.
